parameters.description: Customer ID to fetch organization units for

parameters.description: Maximum number of organization units to return

post.description: Create a new media document for the authenticated customer. currentVersion.mediaPath and currentVersion.sizeBytes are required.
Update top-level metadata (area, associations, nickName, externalID). Does not modify versions.
Add a new version and rotate the currentVersion into previousVersions.

post.summary: Restore a previous version as current
post.description: Promote a version from previousVersions to currentVersion.
Soft delete a media document by setting deletedAt. Requires ownership by customer.
Restore a soft-deleted media document by clearing deletedAt. Requires ownership by customer.
Start a new chunked upload session. Returns session ID for subsequent chunk uploads.
Upload a single chunk for an active upload session. chunkData is base64 encoded; chunk size is determined by /upload/initiate (currently 1.5MB).
Assemble all chunks and create the final media file, then upload to S3.
Abort an active upload session and clean up all chunks.
Hard delete a media document that has been marked as deleted. Cannot be undone.

get.description: Streams the media file for the given media ID. Optionally provide a versionID to download a specific version; otherwise the current version will be served.

get.summary: Internal: proxy to S3 for media streaming
get.description: INTERNAL ONLY. Proxies current media version to Nginx for caching. Requires X-CDS-Internal-Proxy header.

post.summary: Verify cdsBearerAuthStatic access for a path

Validates whether the provided customer sessionID exists and is active. Caller must authenticate with STATIC via x-cds-auth-hint and Authorization header.
